title: araucaria subulata
intro: Araucaria subulata, also known as the Cook pine or Norfolk Island pine, is a species of coniferous evergreen tree native to the subtropical islands of New Caledonia and Norfolk Island. The resin of Araucaria subulata has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to the presence of terpenes and other bioactive compounds. The resin has been used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial and expectorant properties. In herbal preparations, Araucaria subulata resin is often combined with other plant extracts to create topical ointments and infusions for wound care and respiratory health.
